summary_one_line: Take false branch by supplying a well-formed cmap table header so bsearch on (platformID,encodingID) returns a non-zero subtable offset.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt/cmap structure: the leading TrueType header (00 01 00 00) with a 'cmap' table record whose EncodingRecord entries carry a (platformID, encodingID) key matched by bsearch AND a non-zero subtable offset, so result.subtable != 0 and the false branch at line 2027 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal font magic 00 01 00 00 00 0c ... followed by the ASCII tag 'cmap' at offset 0x0c and a 'ttlf' tag, i.e. a structurally valid table directory. This drives encodingRecord.bsearch(key) to a record with a non-zero subtable, so the dereference at line 2030 (return &(this+result.subtable)) executes — marked [W] in the overlay.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are tiny (13-68 bytes) and lack the cmap magic/tag bytes (e.g. e0 17 00 00 1a 20 ...), so no valid EncodingRecord with a subtable offset exists; bsearch returns a record whose subtable is zero and line 2028 (return nullptr) is taken every time (L true:false = 100:0 at L2027).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace: the per-execution CMP buffer captures the comparisons against the magic constant and the EncodingRecord key fields, and I2SRandReplace substitutes those captured operands directly into the input, synthesizing the exact 4-byte magic/tag and matching key bytes. value_profile (loser) lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets CMP_MAP distance buckets, which cannot place the exact multi-byte literals to construct a valid cmap header.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false branch of `if (!result.subtable)`, requiring a non-zero subtable to resolve.
The winner takes the false side resolving to the subtable dereference, marked W-only in the overlay.
Winner seeds carry the font magic 00 01 00 00 and the ASCII 'cmap' tag, a valid table directory.
Loser-blocking seeds are tiny and lack the cmap header bytes.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false side while the loser is 100% true (always returns nullptr).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2027  T=24 F=6  T=100 F=0  if (!result.subtable)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner fuzzer value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace to its mutator stack.
The loser fuzzer value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, only CMP_MAP distance buckets.
The decisive pair is vpc>vp with an I2S delta and full prob_div separation.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5436 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2cd766ba50495565` over a 44-level chain; observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only) at depth 43, the unwrapped-I2S signal exclusive to cmplog/vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on `if (!result.subtable)` after a bsearch over a magic/tag-validated cmap header shows value_profile resolving the false branch as often as value_profile_cmplog (no I2S advantage), refuting the I2SRandReplace attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the exact byte that flips result.subtable to non-zero is the cmap magic literal or instead the EncodingRecord key/subtable-offset field — the byte diff shows both header-magic and later structural offsets differing, so the precise gated operand is not pinned.
